query: Based on emissivity altitude behavior, what is the composition of the tallest Venus volcanoes?

tool_input.summary: Searched the PDS catalog for Venus emissivity datasets relevant to emissivity–altitude analyses. The catalog results most directly matching are Magellan radiometer-derived emissivity products (SCVDR EDF) and the gridded GVDR summaries, both at the PDS Geosciences node (PDS4). No Venus Express/VIRTIS emissivity datasets were returned by these catalog searches in this environment.

candidates.title: Magellan SCVDR Emissivity Data File (EDF) Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Contains Magellan-derived surface microwave emissivity estimates (from thermal radiometry) used in classic emissivity-versus-elevation studies, including analysis of high-elevation/volcano summit emissivity behavior.

candidates.title: Magellan Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Parent bundle for SCVDR products; includes emissivity plus related scattering/roughness parameters that are often analyzed alongside altitude to interpret summit materials.

candidates.title: Magellan GVDR Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Provides gridded/global vector summaries derived from SCVDR (commonly used for correlating emissivity with topography/elevation at regional to global scale, including volcanic highlands).

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
Based on emissivity altitude behavior, what is the composition of the tallest Venus volcanoes?

Magellan SCVDR Emissivity Data File (EDF) Data Collection

dataset.title: Magellan Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) Bundle
dataset.description: The Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) is an orbit-by-orbit reduction of Magellan scattering and emission measurements carried out at Stanford University. The SCVDR includes near-nadir scattering functions obtained by numerical inversion from altimetry (ALT) echoes, results (e.g., rms surface slopes and Fresnel reflectivity) from fitting analytic functions to those inversions, scattering function segments at oblique incidence angles derived from synthetic aperture radar (SAR) echoes, and estimates of surface emissivity derived from thermal microwave radiometry (RAD) measurements. The SCVDR is one of several inputs to the Global Vector Data Record (GVDR), a gridded summary of scattering results, also produced at Stanford.
